items.title: Green shoots after rain give SA farmers hope after years of drought

children.content: Farmers in the Riverland and Mallee are cautiously optimistic as new growth emerges from the paddock following "meaningful rain".

gallery.altText: Green sprouts of barley are shooting up through red soil with farmer's boots in backgrounds.

thumbnail.altText: Green sprouts of barley are shooting up through red soil with farmer's boots in backgrounds.

items.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years

children.content: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

image.altText: A court sketch of Snowtown killer James Vlassakis.

items.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's medication

children.content: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.

image.altText: A sign on a window for CHEMIST WAREHOUSE with another saying STOP PAYING TOO MUCH! and a trolley out the front

items.title: Network 10 to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses

children.content: The end of a broadcast deal between Network 10 and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a brown wall

items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

items.title: Anger as 140-year-old flour mill walls demolished to build hotel despite outcry

children.content: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.

gallery.altText: Heavy machinery claws away the stone wall, which is fenced.
Heavy machinery removing walls of stone structure, rubble in the forefront
Orange heavy machinery remove pieces of stone and timber with heritage structure in the background

thumbnail.altText: Heavy machinery claws away the stone wall, which is fenced.
Heavy machinery removing walls of stone structure, rubble in the forefront
Orange heavy machinery remove pieces of stone and timber with heritage structure in the background

items.title: Power network's $10 million plan to combat SA's repeated regional blackouts

children.content: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

items.title: National Trust SA faces inquiry motion amid $230,000 property dispute

children.content: Another National of Trust South Australia branch goes public about its financial dispute with the high-profile heritage group, as the state opposition announces it will refer members' concerns to a parliamentary committee.

image.altText: A middle aged man in a T shirt standing in front of several historical cars

items.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address

children.content: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

image.altText: Premier Peter Malinauskas is wearing a suit and standing in front of new homes

items.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue

children.content: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.

image.altText: Bonython Hall - an old heritage brick building in the Adelaide CBD

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

articles.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

image.altText: A view of the Whyalla steelworks.

articles.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities

children.content: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

gallery.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

thumbnail.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: 'This is the end': SA health chief recalls terrifying moment she was hit by car

children.content: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

image.altText: A woman pulls the top of her blouse aside, showing a 5-centimetre scar on her left shoulder

items.title: Son arrested in Spain over billionaire fashion tycoon father's deadly 100m cliff fall

children.content: A family spokesperson confirmed Jonathan Andic was being questioned by investigators. His father, Isak Andic, died in December 2024.

image.altText: A man in navy top and navy coat, walks with a police officer, with his head down.

items.title: Life inside one of the world's most dangerous waterways

children.content: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

gallery.altText: women take selfie on red beach.
women sit on ground with paint and canvas making art.

women sit on ground with paint and canvas making art.

items.title: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth dies aged 91

children.content: Peter Hollingworth was the first and only cleric to serve as governor-general, before his resignation in 2003 amid his handling of historical church abuse scandals.

image.altText: Close-up of a man with grey hair looking to right of frame

items.title: Two Italian divers' bodies recovered from underwater cave

children.content: Finnish divers recovered the bodies after they were located deep inside an underwater cave in the Maldives.

image.altText: A diver is seen on the deck of a boat, preparing to go into the water. Diving equipment is on the floor beside them.

items.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?

children.content: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?

gallery.altText: Thermometers on a table with a healthcare worker in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Thermometers on a table with a healthcare worker in the background.

avatar.alt: Close-up of person wearing dark top and green earrings.

items.title: Far-right Israeli minister accuses ICC of making 'secret' request to arrest him

children.content: Reports have been circulating that the ICC is preparing to issue warrants against Israel's far-right finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich.

image.altText: Bezalel Smotrich speaking into a microphone with a flag behind him.

items.title: Developers targeting affluent areas under affordable housing scheme

children.content: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a multi-story building development, alongside a busy road.

items.title: Undersea volcanic eruption visible from space

children.content: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.

image.altText: Plume of smoke on distant ocean horizon.

gallery.altText: A volcano erupts under the ocean sending smoke and ash into the sky

items.title: Complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into embattled mental health clinic

children.content: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.

image.altText: Generic photos of a mental health facility.

items.title: The men offering an alternative to ‘manosphere’ influencers

children.content: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

image.altText: A selfie of Caleb Ferdinand and his toddler, taken inside a car.

items.title: 'Absolute madness': How a costume rock duo are breaking the internet

children.content: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.

items.title: ARIA winner TISM causes $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House

children.content: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with a hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.

items.title: Apology for 'appalling' display of autopsy body parts without consent

children.content: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.

image.altText: Slices of body parts covered in Perspex and put on display on walls.

items.title: Rain 'more than double' forecast drenches Gold Coast amid BOM accuracy concerns

children.content: As flash flooding from record-setting rainfall recedes, some south-east Queensland residents feel that Bureau of Meteorology forecasts fell short of what was experienced.

image.altText: A road closed due to flooding sign in front of a road covered by water.

items.title: Two dead after car hits tree in Dandenong in Melbourne's south-east

children.content: Police say a car was travelling along the Princes Highway when the driver lost control and crashed into a tree about 1:20am.

items.title: Planned upgrades to aging submarine fleet scaled back

children.content: A massive program of upgrades to Australia's aging fleet of Collins Class submarines, aimed at keeping them in the water a decade longer, is being scaled back.

items.title: Israeli forces have detained 11 Australians after intercepting Gaza-bound flotilla

children.content: Israeli forces detain 11 Australians after its navy intercepts ships seeking to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza, flotilla organisers say.

image.altText: A woman sitting on a ship holding a sign calling for someone's freedom, with Palestinian flags flying in background.

items.title: What we know about the deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque

children.content: Two teenagers opened fire at the largest mosque in San Diego, California on Monday, killing three men. Here is what we know about the attack.

items.title: Tasmania's footy turf war is over, but the Devils must now deliver

children.content: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.
